content.full_text:
-

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 24, 1994

Beth Reno, Treasurer
Steve Peace for Senate
P. O. Box 120190
Chula Vista, CA 91912

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-040

Dear Ms. Reno:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice on behalf
of the Steve Peace for Senate Committee concerning the campaign
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

You inquired as to what actions should be taken by the Steve
Peace for Senate Committee since the committee received
notification from Zenith Insurance Company that the company
reimbursed one of its executives for a $100 contribution the
executive made at a fundraiser that was held on September 27,
1993. The Steve Peace for Senate Committee reports Gerald Curtin
as the contributor on the committee's second pre-election
statement in connection with the November 2, 1993 special
election. In addition, you stated that the Steve Peace for Senate
Committee received a transfer of funds from the regular Assembly
committee on October 29, 1993. A $1,000 contribution that was
attributed to Zenith Insurance Company was included in this
transfer.

Your letter correctly summarizes my telephone advice to amend
the second pre-election statement to reflect Gerald Curtin as an
intermediary for the $100 contribution received from Zenith
Insurance Company. A person is an intermediary for a contribution
if the recipient of the contribution would consider the person to
be the contributor without the disclosure of the identity of the
true source of the contribution. (Regulation 18432.5.)

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Ms. Reno
Our File No. A-94-040
Page Two

     Since the Assembly committee had previously transferred funds
to the Steve Peace for Senate Committee that included a $1,000
contribution attributed to Zenith Insurance Company, you were
advised that the Steve Peace for Senate Committee should return
$100 to the Assembly committee.  During a special election cycle,
the maximum allowable contribution from a "person" is $1,000.  The
term "person" includes a company. (Sections 82047 and 85305.)

     If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        by: Dixie Howard
                                            Political Reform Consultant

sections.question: What actions should be taken by the Steve Peace for Senate Committee since the committee received notification from Zenith Insurance Company that the company reimbursed one of its executives for a $100 contribution the executive made at a fundraiser that was held on September 27, 1993?
sections.conclusion: The Steve Peace for Senate Committee should amend the second pre-election statement to reflect Gerald Curtin as an intermediary for the $100 contribution received from Zenith Insurance Company. The committee should return $100 to the Assembly committee because the maximum allowable contribution from a person (including a company) during a special election cycle is $1,000, and Zenith Insurance Company's contributions exceeded this limit.
sections.question_synthetic: The Steve Peace for Senate Committee received a $100 contribution from an executive that was reimbursed by Zenith Insurance Company, and also received a $1,000 transfer attributed to Zenith Insurance Company. What disclosure and reporting corrections are required?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The committee must amend its pre-election statement to name Gerald Curtin as an intermediary, and return $100 to the Assembly committee since Zenith Insurance Company's total contributions ($1,100) exceeded the $1,000 maximum per election cycle.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard FPPC advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document addresses campaign disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act. No withdrawal or declination language present.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What actions should be taken by the Steve Peace for Senate Committee since the committee received notification from Zenith Insurance Company that the company reimbursed one of its executives for a $100 contribution the executive made at a fundraiser that was held on September 27, 1993?

CONCLUSION: The Steve Peace for Senate Committee should amend the second pre-election statement to reflect Gerald Curtin as an intermediary for the $100 contribution received from Zenith Insurance Company. The committee should return $100 to the Assembly committee because the maximum allowable contribution from a person (including a company) during a special election cycle is $1,000, and Zenith Insurance Company's contributions exceeded this limit.

The Steve Peace for Senate Committee reports Gerald Curtin as the contributor on the committee's second pre-election statement in connection with the November 2, 1993 special election. Your letter correctly summarizes my telephone advice to amend the second pre-election statement to reflect Gerald Curtin as an intermediary for the $100 contribution received from Zenith Insurance Company. embedding.summary: The FPPC confirmed telephone advice to the Steve Peace for Senate Committee regarding the proper reporting and handling of a $100 contribution reimbursed by Zenith Insurance Company, including the requirement to designate an intermediary and return excess funds to comply with contribution limits.
